silver-fixture

version 0.1.0  /  declared tier: convergent

02

Executive summary

A derived, plain-language read of the deterministic result.

How to read this report

The colored matrix above is the whole verdict in one glance: every check is a chip, color-coded pass / fail / warn / not-applicable, grouped by tier. The tier is decided by a deterministic gate with a real exit code, not by opinion. Everything below expands that picture.

silver-fixture declares the Silver (Convergent) tier and earns Silver. Of the 29 checks in the spine, 26 do not fail (22 pass, 1 warn, 3 not applicable) and 3 fail. The deterministic gate exits 0.

3 requirement(s) block Gold: G2, G5, G4. Section 06 orders the climb; section 07 gives a copy-paste fix for each.

1 advisory warning(s) surfaced (G8); they do not gate but are worth folding in.

03

What was evaluated

The subject identity, then the component inventory.

Subject
silver-fixture
Version
0.1.0
Declared tier
Convergent (Silver)
Agent targets
claude, codex
Prefix
sf-
Grading profile
askit-library
ComponentTypeVersion
sf-do-thingskill0.1.0
04

Methodology and scope

Three layers, kept separate so the verdict stays honest. Only the deterministic gate decides the tier.

Layer 1 - Conformance

Decides the tier. The portable Node gate runs every check with a real exit code, no model in the loop. Re-run node scripts/check.mjs . to reproduce it.

Layer 2 - Behavioral

Advisory only. A skill is run against its eval set; results are evidence and never decide a gate result.

Layer 3 - Review

Advisory only. A qualitative pass over scoping and design; it informs the insights, it does not move the badge.

Status legend

Pass requirement metFail blocks the tierWarn advisoryN/A vacuous pass

Confidence and limitations

Conformance findings are exact and reproducible: the same gate on the same commit yields the same result. Vacuous passes (U11, G6, G1) mean there was nothing to validate, not that a feature was exercised.

05

Tier compliance - evidence ledger

One row per requirement, all 29 itemized and grouped by tier. Every non-pass carries a why-it-matters note.

BronzeTier satisfied.11 / 11

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/library-json.mjs

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/anatomy.mjs

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/per-target-presence.mjs

Gold7 satisfied, 3 block the tier.7 / 10
G3library-regressionPass

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/library-regression.mjs

G6deprecationN/A

Nothing to validate for this subject (vacuous pass). checks/deprecation.mjs

G1hook-documentationN/A

Nothing to validate for this subject (vacuous pass). checks/hook-documentation.mjs

G2self-hostingFail

no CI workflow under .github/workflows/; Gold requires self-hosting CI that runs the conformance gate (Standard sec 2.6 G2, sec 4). .github/workflows/ checks/self-hosting.mjs

Why it mattersWithout CI running the gate, conformance is a claim, not a proof. Any change can silently regress the library, and a consumer cannot point to a green badge that says the standard held on the latest commit.
G5release-notesFail

RELEASE-NOTES.md is missing at the repository root; Gold requires a curated, user-facing RELEASE-NOTES.md distinct from CHANGELOG.md (Standard sec 2.6 G5, sec 10.6). RELEASE-NOTES.md checks/release-notes.mjs

Why it mattersA changelog is for maintainers; release notes are for users. Without a curated RELEASE-NOTES.md, adopters have no human-readable summary of what changed and why they should upgrade.
G4index-driftFail

INDEX.md is missing; at Gold it is generated by gen-index and drift-checked (Standard sec 2.6 G4). Generate it: node scripts/generators/gen-index.mjs . --write INDEX.md checks/index-drift.mjs

Why it mattersA stale INDEX.md misrepresents the library to anyone reading the catalog; generating it and drift-checking in CI keeps the published index honest on every commit.
G7docs-frontmatterPass

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/docs-frontmatter.mjs

G8folder-readmeWarn

meaningful folder has no README.md (ADR 0024 D1.1); scaffold one with askit-build-docs folder-readme mode. skills/sf-do-thing/README.md checks/folder-readme.mjs

Why it mattersA meaningful folder with no README leaves a reader guessing what it holds; a short folder guide is the cheapest orientation a contributor or consumer gets.
G9source-docPass

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/source-doc.mjs

G10docs-presencePass

Requirement satisfied; no finding raised. checks/docs-presence.mjs

06

The climb / burndown

Exactly what blocks Gold, ordered as a worklist.

G2no CI workflow under .github/workflows/

no CI workflow under .github/workflows/; Gold requires self-hosting CI that runs the conformance gate (Standard sec 2.6 G2, sec 4).
Effort~20 min

G5RELEASE-NOTES.md is missing at the repository root

RELEASE-NOTES.md is missing at the repository root; Gold requires a curated, user-facing RELEASE-NOTES.md distinct from CHANGELOG.md (Standard sec 2.6 G5, sec 10.6).
Effort~15 min

G4INDEX.md is missing

INDEX.md is missing; at Gold it is generated by gen-index and drift-checked (Standard sec 2.6 G4). Generate it: node scripts/generators/gen-index.mjs . --write
Effort~5 min

After these, re-run node scripts/check.mjs .; the Gold tier should clear.

07

Improvement path

One card per gap: the issue, priority and effort, and a copy-paste prompt that drives the matching askit builder and re-runs the gate.

G2

self-hosting

Priority high~20 min
Issue: no CI workflow under .github/workflows/; Gold requires self-hosting CI that runs the conformance gate (Standard sec 2.6 G2, sec 4).
Copy-paste promptUse askit-build-settings (CI mode) on this plugin: add .github/workflows/ci.yml that checks out the repo, sets up Node, runs npm ci, then runs node scripts/check.mjs as a required step. Then run node scripts/check.mjs and confirm G2 passes.
G5

release-notes

Priority high~15 min
Issue: RELEASE-NOTES.md is missing at the repository root; Gold requires a curated, user-facing RELEASE-NOTES.md distinct from CHANGELOG.md (Standard sec 2.6 G5, sec 10.6).
Copy-paste promptUse askit-release (notes mode) to create a curated, user-facing RELEASE-NOTES.md distinct from CHANGELOG.md. Then run node scripts/check.mjs and confirm G5 passes.
G4

index-drift

Priority high~5 min
Issue: INDEX.md is missing; at Gold it is generated by gen-index and drift-checked (Standard sec 2.6 G4). Generate it: node scripts/generators/gen-index.mjs . --write
Copy-paste promptRegenerate INDEX.md: run node scripts/generators/gen-index.mjs . --write. Then run node scripts/check.mjs and confirm G4 passes.
G8

folder-readme

Priority medium~15 min
Issue: meaningful folder has no README.md (ADR 0024 D1.1); scaffold one with askit-build-docs folder-readme mode.
Copy-paste promptUse askit-build-docs (folder-readme mode) on skills/sf-do-thing/README.md: scaffold a README.md for the meaningful folder. Then run node scripts/check.mjs and confirm G8 passes.
08

Insights

None for a deterministic conformance report.

No insights in a conformance report

Insights are produced by the advisory review layer (askit-reviewer), which a deterministic conformance report does not run. Run review mode to populate this section; a conformance report does not fabricate qualitative notes.

09

Evidence and sources

Citations grounding the findings: the check module, Standard clause, or subject file.

  • CHECKscripts/check.mjs - the portable deterministic gate (node scripts/check.mjs .); its exit code drives the verdict.
  • MODULEchecks/self-hosting.mjs - the check behind a non-pass row above.
  • MODULEchecks/release-notes.mjs - the check behind a non-pass row above.
  • MODULEchecks/index-drift.mjs - the check behind a non-pass row above.
  • MODULEchecks/folder-readme.mjs - the check behind a non-pass row above.
  • CLAUSEStandard v0.8 - the 29-check spine and the Bronze / Silver / Gold tier definitions.
  • FILElibrary.json - the subject identity (name, version, tier, agent-targets, prefix).

Generated by the askit-evaluate report renderer. The conformance layer is deterministic and reproducible; re-run node scripts/check.mjs . to reproduce every finding. This report adds no judgment and does not change the verdict.
